10-year-old shoot mom over VR headset
Fire crews respond to fire at boarded up building
Milwaukee Dancing Grannies planning return
HS football players gain perspective helping vets
She made a promise to her husband to keep their tree farm going
Almost 200 animals rescued from puppy mill
Water study could delay commercial construction
Girl who called 911 for her father honored
Hospitals becoming a 'dumping ground' for kids
Christmas parade road closures and detours
Cars totaling nearly $200k stolen from dealership
Mother fends off raccoon from daughter
Car chase ends with police-involved shooting
$200,000 worth of supplies donated to PB schools
Family throws surprise birthday party at Eagles tailgate for Vietnam vet
Egypt wins in World Cup knockout round debut, edges out Australia on penalties
items.summary: Hossam Abdelmaguid scored the deciding goal in a shootout, and Egypt won its debut in the World Cup knockout round by beating Australia 4-2 on penalty kicks after a 1-1 draw Friday.

items.title: Trump delivers remarks at Mount Rushmore to kick off America’s 250th anniversary
items.summary: Watch live coverage as President Trump delivers remarks at Mount Rushmore before a fireworks display, kicking off a weekend of 250th-anniversary celebrations across the country.

items.title: Messi brilla con un control y una definición preciosa para anotar el primero de Argentina
items.summary: Lisandro Martínez envió un pase filtrado al área y Lionel Messi hizo un gol de élite, controlando el esférico en el área chica. Con esto llega a 7 anotaciones en el torneo y le da ventaja de 1-0 a Argentina sobre Cabo Verde en dieciseisavos.

items.title: Lionel Scaloni cumple 100 partidos con la Selección de Argentina | Enlace Mundial
items.summary: El exjugador argentino, Maxi Rodríguez, destaca el trabajo del entrenador Scaloni por la regularidad y palmarés más grande para un entrenador de la ‘albiceleste’. Scaloni, dice Rodríguez, es el primero del equipo en proponer el máximo rendimiento.

items.title: Valdano: “No sé que tiene que hacer Cabo Verde para tomarla en serio” | Enlace Mundial
items.summary: Jorge Valdano afirma que la selección argentina no ha subestimado a ningún equipo a los que se han enfrentado en este Mundial y pronostica que un buen juego de Luis Díaz puede dar la ventaja a Colombia.

items.title: Batistuta ve el tiempo del partido de hoy como la mayor dificultad de Argentina | Enlace Mundial
items.summary: Batistuta cree que Argentina debe enfrentar estos dieciseisavos con seriedad, pero resalta el hecho de que Argentina sabe enfrentar partidos con selecciones de distintos niveles de calidad.

items.title: Messi va por el bicampeonato: Argentina calienta para chocar con Cabo Verde
items.summary: La albiceleste inicia su calentamiento para el duelo ante Cabo Verde, equipo revelación del Mundial, mientras que Argentina parte como amplio favorito de la mano de Lionel Messi.

items.title: Salah llora de felicidad tras la victoria de Egipto sobre Australia
items.summary: Egipto acaba de conseguir la victoria más importante de su historia en la Copa del Mundo tras imponerse en penales a Australia en la ronda de 16vos. Su capitán y referente, Mohamed Salah, habló tras el partido y conmovió a todos con sus palabras.

items.title: Egipto vence a Australia en penales y sella su pase a los Octavos de Final del Mundial 2026.
items.summary: Egipto sobrevivió a una dramática tanda de penales y aseguró su boleto a los Octavos de Final del Mundial 2026. En la siguiente ronda se medirá al ganador del duelo entre Argentina y Cabo Verde.

items.title: ‘Dibu’ Martínez luce nuevo look para la primera ronda de eliminatorias
items.summary: Emiliano ‘Dibu’ Martínez luce la bandera albiceleste en el cabello para el duelo clave ante Cabo Verde en Miami. Scaloni define el once titular de Argentina, con el regreso de Romero y Lautaro de arranque, buscando sellar el boleto frente a Cabo Verde.

items.title: Roberto Ayala: "Tenemos que encontrar los espacios para ganar ante Cabo Verde"
items.summary: Roberto Ayala, asistente técnico en la selección de Argentina, destacó que el equipo llega preparado a todas las situaciones que se puedan enfrentar en el juego ante Cabo Verde, sabiendo que deberán buscar espacios para anotar.

items.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce fans gather for glimpse of top secret wedding
items.summary: Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. The wedding bash is expected to last into Saturday morning.

items.title: Juan Musso sobre el juego Argentina vs Cabo Verde: "Tenemos al mejor y al más humilde de todos"
items.summary: El portero suplente de la selección argentina se refirió al juego decisivo que van a enfrentar contra Cabo Verde, destacando que estarán concentrados con humildad en su talento y preparación para llevarse la victoria.

items.title: Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz remains a powerful bargaining chip
items.summary: Despite efforts by U.S. negotiators, Iran says it wants to charge a toll for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. It's yet another unresolved issue of the U.S.-Iran war.

items.title: Arizona toddler declared dead was found alive in the morgue
items.summary: The 18-month-old boy was pronounced dead after a near-drowning but was found breathing hours later in a hospital morgue.

items.title: U.S. was concerned Israel might try to kill Iran negotiators, sources say
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu disavowed reports of the U.S. concerns as “a complete fabrication of reality.”

items.title: A family steps in to save the dying casino town of Primm, Nevada
items.summary: Primm, Nev., a once-thriving casino town on the border with California, was on the verge of fading away for good. The family it was named for has stepped in and faces the challenge of reviving it.

items.title: Iran plans dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei after war death
items.summary: The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.

items.title: Record heat disrupts America’s 250th birthday celebrations and travel
items.summary: A record-setting heat wave is blanketing much of the U.S. as millions of Americans gather for Independence Day, prompting the cancellation of events marking America’s 250th birthday.

items.title: Graphics: The evolution of Taylor Swift’s music
items.summary: Across 16 albums, the pop superstar’s lyrics have traced a journey from romance and heartbreak to marriage, lasting commitment and a new chapter with Travis Kelce.

items.title: Preliminary report didn't flag an engine failure before a skydiving plane crash that killed 12
items.summary: A preliminary report issued by federal safety investigators didn't flag any serious safety failures that could have led to the fiery crash of a plane last month on a skydiving outing in Missouri that killed all 12 people aboard

items.title: Wildfire southwest of Denver forces thousands to evacuate, destroys 160 structures
items.summary: A wildfire burning southwest of Denver has forced the evacuation of thousands of residents and destroyed more than 160 structures by Friday as erratic winds pushed the blaze across two Colorado counties

items.title: Louisiana's top court halts the criminal case against the state attorney general
items.summary: Louisiana's Supreme Court has halted for now the criminal case against state Attorney General Liz Murrill

items.title: Mamdani offers a contrast to Trump’s vision for America in a 250th anniversary address
items.summary: The New York mayor gave a solidly pro-immigration speech, flanked by recently naturalized citizens, hours before the president plans to give his own address at Mount Rushmore.

items.title: From The Sports Desk: Portugal advances, Croatia sent packing after dramatic finish
items.summary: Portugal advanced after edging out Croatia 2-1 in a hard-fought match, and Cristiano Ronaldo’s team will now face European champion Spain on Monday.

items.title: Tribes hope Farm Bill can feed more people and preserve Indigenous culture
items.summary: Dawn and Cassius Spears would like to expand their farm’s reach beyond their farm stand, but it’s challenging

items.title: Rare copy of the Declaration of Independence seized by the British in 1776 is found
items.summary: A rare copy of the Declaration of Independence lost for 250 years has been discovered in London, where it is the only known example of its kind outside the U.S.

items.title: July 4th events threatened by heat wave. And, Russia strikes on Ukraine's capital
items.summary: July 4th events for America's milestone birthday are being threatened by a brutal heat wave. And, Russia has struck Ukraine's capital, killing several people in what it calls retaliatory attacks.

items.title: Preparations Underway for Funeral of Iran’s Supreme Leader
items.summary: Massive crowds are expected to gather for a dayslong funeral for Iran’s former Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei, who was killed in a U.S. strike on the first day of the war. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

items.title: Just gave birth or are about to? Share your story and questions about costs
items.summary: The costs of having a baby in the U.S. can be hard to predict and budget for. We want to hear your stories and questions about how to navigate the system.

items.title: Former USAID head grieves its closure while hoping for its future
items.summary: Former USAID administrator reflects on a year since USAID's shutdown and the new direction of US foreign aid.

items.title: But first, coffee: The drink that energized the American Revolution
items.summary: Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.

items.title: How a fertilizer shortage caused by the Iran war could affect U.S. food prices
items.summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupted shipments of fertilizer and natural gas, a key component in fertilizer manufacturing. It's unlikely to cause major price hikes for U.S. grocery shoppers.

items.title: Oregon ER doctors win a 'David and Goliath' battle against a national company
items.summary: In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.

items.title: What to know about the challenges ahead for the children rescued from an Ohio home
items.summary: The 16 children rescued a Ohio home in deplorable conditions face a long road toward recovery, experts say

items.title: Possible super typhoon threatens US Pacific territories still recovering from last storm
items.summary: Residents of U.S. territories in the western Pacific are bracing for a possible super typhoon, just months after the region was hit by the strongest tropical cyclone on Earth this year

items.title: Trump's ties to Erdogan sold him on this year's NATO summit. Turkey may win big in other ways
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has berated and belittled many of the European leaders expected to attend next week’s NATO summit in Turkey but has only praise for its host, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan

items.title: Latin Mass, but faithful to the pope: A Minnesota church shows a different way during schism crisis
items.summary: Priests at the Church of Saint Agnes in St. Paul, Minnesota, celebrate a traditional Latin Rite Mass every Sunday, even though the practice has become a symbol of cultural and political battles among some Catholics

items.title: Man with a Tibetan flag dies after setting himself on fire in front of UN HQ
items.summary: Officials say a man holding a Tibetan flag in front of the United Nations headquarters in New York set himself on fire and died

items.title: ¡Golazo de Gonçalo Ramos al 93' pone en ventaja a Portugal!
items.summary: ¡Locura total al minuto 93! Rafael Leão mandó un centro con comba perfecta desde la banda izquierda y Gonçalo Ramos conectó un brutal giro de cuello para clavar el balón en las redes, sellando el 2-1 de Portugal sobre Croacia en el cierre del encuentro.

items.title: Maine couple spots a bear chasing a moose calf and helps it escape
items.summary: A Maine couple had an unexpected wildlife encounter while heading out for a day of fishing

items.title: Puyallup Tribe and First Nations in Canada hope World Cup partnerships raise cultural awareness
items.summary: A handful of Indigenous tribes are partnering with World Cup host cities to showcase their traditions and cultures

items.title: Massachusetts city with large Cape Verdean community sets curfew ahead of World Cup match
items.summary: A Massachusetts city home to one of the nation’s largest Cape Verdean communities will impose a nighttime curfew ahead of Cape Verde’s World Cup match against Argentina

items.title: Tiger Woods’ hospital records will be handed over to prosecutors in Florida DUI case, judge rules
items.summary: A judge has ruled that prosecutors are allowed to review medical records related to Tiger Woods’ March vehicle crash and subsequent arrest in Florida on suspicion of driving under the influence

items.title: The Supreme Court tackled race, history and the law in fraught and reflective major rulings
items.summary: The Supreme Court has wrapped up a term with significant rulings on race and discrimination

items.title: Wall Street rises toward the finish of its best week in nearly 2 months after bond yields relax
items.summary: U.S. stocks are holding relatively steady after the latest update on the job market suggested the Federal Reserve may feel less pressure to hike interest rates

items.title: Shark attack on Alabama teen inspires the start of a national alert system
items.summary: Lulu Gribbin survived a near-fatal shark attack in 2024

items.title: Ransom notes in Nancy Guthrie case still being investigated as legitimate, FBI says
items.summary: The FBI has discounted some of the ransom notes that surfaced after the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie as extortion attempts

items.title: Tropical storm Douglas forms in Pacific Ocean, no threat to land: Hurricane center
items.summary: Tropical Storm Douglas has formed in the Pacific Ocean

items.title: Tropical Storm Douglas has formed in the Pacific Ocean
items.summary: Tropical Storm Douglas has formed in the Pacific Ocean

items.title: Head of the Chicago Police Department to retire after 3 years in the position
items.summary: The head of the Chicago Police Department has announced that he will retire after a short tenure leading the nation’s second-largest police force

items.title: John Dickinson’s missing 1776 signature haunts his legacy
items.summary: Jane Calvert has spent 25 years championing John Dickinson, a lesser-known Revolutionary War figure

items.title: Alibaba to pay US $600M to settle allegations it allowed illegal sales
items.summary: Alibaba has agreed to pay $600 million to settle a dispute with the U.S. government

items.title: Trump administration sues California, Virginia over new gun laws
items.summary: The Trump administration has sued California and Virginia over new laws in both states that restrict the sale of semiautomatic firearms

items.title: Challengers score victories in lawsuit against Arkansas' restrictions on citizen ballot initiatives
items.summary: A federal judge in Arkansas is throwing out some state laws that put extra restrictions on efforts to gather signatures for ballot initiatives

items.title: Sweeping review of grand jury presentations underway in Chicago following misconduct revelations
items.summary: Justice Department officials are conducting a sweeping review of more than 1,000 grand jury presentations made by Illinois prosecutors following the dismissal of a high-profile case over misconduct

items.title: The best 4th of July sales to shop before the holiday weekend
items.summary: The best 4th of July sales are happening now. Our editors found worthwhile deals on Apple, Yeti, Medicube and more.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
